prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_catboost', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: FT_catboost leads trend tracking with lowest slope error (0.7133), while THETA dominates seasonality capture (0.7637) and overall RMSE (988.28). Early horizon favors DWT_catboost, late horizon prefers ONLY_CWT_rf—different models excel at different forecast steps. With rmse_spread_ratio=0.33 (>0.3) AND seasonality_corr_variance=1.21 (>0.3), both high disagreement and seasonal variance conditions are met, making DBA combination the optimal approach to leverage complementary model strengths.
prompts.skeptic:

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "DWT_catboost", "late": "ONLY_CWT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "FT_catboost", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "CWT_svr", "DWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["ARIMA", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "FT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "DWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.3348,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "FT_catboost leads trend tracking with lowest slope error (0.7133), while THETA dominates seasonality capture (0.7637) and overall RMSE (988.28). Early horizon favors DWT_catboost, late horizon prefers ONLY_CWT_rf—different models excel at different forecast steps. With rmse_spread_ratio=0.33 (>0.3) AND seasonality_corr_variance=1.21 (>0.3), both high disagreement and seasonal variance conditions are met, making DBA combination the optimal approach to leverage complementary model strengths."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "balanced",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. rmse_spread_ratio=0.33 and seasonality_corr_variance=1.21 justify DBA combination for handling phase shifts and seasonal variance. FT_catboost (RMSE=1006.09) is trend champion while THETA (RMSE=1015.17) dominates seasonality—dba_combination leverages both. topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 with shrinkage=0.25 provides stability with few windows (n_windows=3). best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific best performers."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination). All candidates are safe from leakage (selection/weighted use past data only; baseline uses no fitting). Performance is balanced: inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 leads with score 0.6126 and RMSE 929.40, while dba_combination has highest composite score 0.6466. No redundancy detected (no ≥4 same-type candidates). Universe leaderboards show poly_weighted_average_p2.0_trim0.8 with better RMSE but lower overall score - current set prioritizes composite score which is correct. n_windows=3 meets threshold for weighted strategies with learns_weights:true.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: FT_catboost leads trend tracking with lowest slope error (0.7133), while THETA dominates seasonality capture (0.7637) and overall RMSE (988.28). Early horizon favors DWT_catboost, late horizon prefers ONLY_CWT_rf—different models excel at different forecast steps. With rmse_spread_ratio=0.33 (>0.3) AND seasonality_corr_variance=1.21 (>0.3), both high disagreement and seasonal variance conditions are met, making DBA combination the optimal approach to leverage complementary model strengths.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. rmse_spread_ratio=0.33 and seasonality_corr_variance=1.21 justify DBA combination for handling phase shifts and seasonal variance. FT_catboost (RMSE=1006.09) is trend champion while THETA (RMSE=1015.17) dominates seasonality—dba_combination leverages both. topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 with shrinkage=0.25 provides stability with few windows (n_windows=3). best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific best performers.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination). All candidates are safe from leakage (selection/weighted use past data only; baseline uses no fitting). Performance is balanced: inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 leads with score 0.6126 and RMSE 929.40, while dba_combination has highest composite score 0.6466. No redundancy detected (no ≥4 same-type candidates). Universe leaderboards show poly_weighted_average_p2.0_trim0.8 with better RMSE but lower overall score - current set prioritizes composite score which is correct. n_windows=3 meets threshold for weighted strategies with learns_weights:true.
